name: The Black Anvil of the Unquiet Engine

description.value:
Properties and Powers

1. Advanced Necromantic Knowledge (Ex)
The Black Anvil contains instructions on creating advanced undead constructs. A necromancer using this book as a reference while crafting undead gains the following benefits:

  • Time Reduction: The crafting time for undead is reduced by 25%.

  • Unique Templates: The book provides access to special templates for undead, including:

    • Necromechanical: Adds mechanical features like chains, sawblades, or reinforced plating (+2 AC, +2 Strength, and +10 ft. movement speed).
    • Engine of Undeath: Grants a construct the ability to perform complex labor or multiple simultaneous tasks (gain an additional attack during full attacks or +10 to Craft/Profession checks).
    • Terror Wrought: Causes the undead to emit a frightful presence (Will save DC 18 or become frightened for 1d4 rounds).

2. Enchanted Preservation (Su)
The tome is imbued with potent magical protections:

  • Nondetection: The book is under a constant Nondetection effect (CL 15th, DC 25 to overcome).
  • Indestructibility: The tome is immune to damage from mundane sources and fire. Destroying it requires an Erase spell cast within a Hallow effect, followed by reducing the book to ashes with consecrated acid.
  • Tamperproof: Attempts to magically alter its text fail, but mundane additions to the margins or blank pages at the back are possible, making it a strange hybrid of preserved original knowledge and user customization.

3. Whispered Guidance (Su)
Those who study the book for 8 hours receive occasional "whispers" from the Tyrant's lingering influence, granting insights into their craft. The reader gains a +4 bonus to Knowledge (Arcana) checks related to necromancy for 24 hours but must succeed at a Will save (DC 20) or suffer 1 point of Wisdom damage from the overwhelming presence.

4. Aura of Dread (Su)
While open, the tome exudes a faint aura of fear within 20 feet. Living creatures must make a Will save (DC 19) or become shaken for as long as they remain in the area. This effect can be suppressed by closing the book.

Curse

The book's knowledge comes at a cost:

  • Corruption of Purpose: Every week the book is studied, the user must make a Will save (DC 22). On a failed save, they become obsessed with creating increasingly horrific undead, gaining 1 point of corruption. At 3 corruption points, their alignment shifts one step closer to evil, and they become fanatically loyal to the Whispering Way's ideals.

Contents

The Black Anvil is divided into several chapters, each focusing on a different type of undead construction:

  1. Bone and Steel: Techniques for fusing skeletal remains with mechanical components.
  2. Necrotic Labor Engines: Methods to create laboring undead constructs such as loggers, miners, or siege workers.
  3. War Machines of Flesh: Instructions for assembling hybrid undead designed for combat, including siege-breakers and terror-spreaders.
  4. The Whispering Choir: Rituals for creating undead that can channel multiple voices for spellcasting or psychic effects.
  5. User Annotations: Dozens of scrawled notes and supplemental diagrams from past owners, ranging from bizarre experiments to chilling improvements on the book’s original designs.
description.unidentified: Bound in dark, unyielding leather with black iron cornerplates, the book radiates an aura of dread and fascination.
